parsed_review.summary:
CP00-467 is a descriptor-only RP15 analytics-core pack (40 units, RP15.P06.M04.S03-RP15.P06.M05.S20: full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail + M05 Permission And Audit Binding head). Review verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS (1 P3, non-blocking).

Verified core claims: (1) production_ready is correctly false/runtime_ready false; implementation_layer is 'descriptor' and the Hermes packet exposes only production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid), so there is no production/enterprise-trust overclaim. (2) Counts are internally consistent across closeout-pack-plan.json, contracts/analytics-core-contract.json, registry.js (ANALYTICS_CORE_CP467_REQUIREMENTS), validators.js, manifest.json, and model.test.js: 40 units, micro_phase rows M04:20/M05:20, deliverable distribution {implementation:15, security_audit:9, ui:8, claude_review:2, test:6} (sums to 40), micro_title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:20, Permission And Audit Binding:20}; deviation_from_plan:false is supported. (3) No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced by freezeCp467Result (all runtime/write/storage/audit/permission flags false; synthetic_only/no_real_data true) and asserted by validators (descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, raw_payload_included:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true) and tests; command-evidence reports all gates exit 0, production_ready:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, git diff --check clean. (4) Claude remains a read-only reviewer, not final approver: authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval:false, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob only. (5) Hermes (H15) is used only for the deterministic descriptor review packet/receipt (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. (6) Review hardening: forbidden_review_evidence encodes auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing-reference, and final-approval/enterprise-trust rejections; readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid JSON, non-zero stdout); validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present:false, summary_only_review_allowed:false (correct for Risk B with security/review deliverables), source_inspection_mode read_tools_used. The absent claude-review-result.json / adjudication.md / construction-inspection.json (and review raw-output/receipt) are expected post-review artifacts per hardened_review_sequence, not gaps. Changed-file scope correctly isolates the 7 modified analytics files (+ index.js reference); unrelated dirty/untracked files (docx, workbook/*, ldip invalid-attempt) are correctly excluded and were not treated as in-scope.

One P3: the README CP00-467 narrative calls this 'the full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice cycle (twenty rows)', but CP00-466 already implemented the M04 head (S01-S02), so this pack covers the 20-row M04 tail (S03-S22) of a 22-row micro-phase — a prose imprecision only; all machine-readable descriptors are correct.

findings.title: README CP00-467 entry mislabels the M04 tail as the 'full M04 cycle'
findings.details: The CP00-467 README section states it covers 'the full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice cycle (twenty rows)'. CP00-466 already implemented the M04 head (S01-S02: 'Permission matrix row', 'View decision binding'; see registry.js ANALYTICS_CORE_CP466_REQUIREMENTS and CP00-466 README), and CP00-467 covers M04.S03-S22 — the 20-row tail of a 22-row micro-phase, not the full cycle. This contradicts the README's own convention, where analogous coverage is described as a 'tail (the N remaining rows)' and 'full ... cycle' is reserved for the 22-row case (e.g., CP00-455 and CP00-464 entries). The discrepancy is confined to README prose: registry ANALYTICS_CORE_CP467_REQUIREMENTS (M04 required_section_rows = 20 titles, S03-S22), contracts/analytics-core-contract.json current_pack and p06_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-467/manifest.json micro_phase_row_counts (RP15.P06.M04:20), validators.js, and model.test.js all correctly model M04 as a 20-row contribution. No counts, coverage, or boundary claim is affected.
findings.recommendation: Reword the CP00-467 README entry to 'the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the twenty remaining rows)' to match the established convention and the upstream CP00-466 head description. Non-blocking; can be remediated in a follow-up pack's README pass, as was done for the CP00-454 stale-label P3 (remediated in CP00-455).
